title: dombeya decanthera subsp. latipetala
intro: Dombeya decanthera subsp. latipetala, a plant species within the Sterculiaceae family, is commonly known as the pink rock rose or wild hibiscus. It is a flowering plant native to South Africa, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's health benefits include treating various conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-aging properties. Traditionally, Dombeya decanthera subsp. latipetala is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, with some herbalists also using it in topical applications for skin and wound care.
